data.title: Kilroy Realty acquires Maple Plaza for $205M
data.description:
• Kilroy Realty (NYSE:KRC) announced on Thursday that it has acquired Maple Plaza, a 293,000-square-foot class A office campus in the iconic Beverly Hills submarket of Los Angeles, for $205 million.
  • The acquisition was funded with cash on hand and recent disposition proceeds, marking Kilroy’s first acquisition in Beverly Hills.
  • Maple Plaza is approximately 75% leased to a diverse mix of tenants across private equity, professional services, education, and entertainment, and has experienced strong leasing activity, with 45,000 square feet signed over the past 12 months, said the company. 
  • KRC +1.28% after hours to $44.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hassett backs Fed's quarter-point rate cut, calling it 'a good first step'
data.description:
White House if National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett cheered the Federal Reserve's decision to cut its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points, calling the move "a good first step in the right direction to much lower rates.”

In an interview with CNBC, Hassett, who is among the names on President Donald Trump’s shortlist for the next Fed chair, noted that new Fed Governor Stephen Miran sought a more aggressive 50-bp reduction. Miran's push for a half-point cut was outvoted 11-1 on the rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee.

Miran's assessment was "heartfelt — it’s not dependent on any partisan influence from the White House," the NEC chief said, adding Miran is going to be "an independent Fed governor." Trump, who nominated Miran, has been calling for the Fed to slash borrowing costs for months.

Hassett, speaking a day after the FOMC cut the policy rate for the first time this year, is backing a "prudent" approach to policy decisions.

He specifically said the Fed should "be looking at all the models, to have a diversity of opinions and to decide, what are we going to do in this economy that really looks to be taking off with inflation that’s decelerating but higher than target."

Hassett was on a tear last week about Fed independence.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bullish sentiment rebounds sharply after three consecutive weekly declines, shows latest AAII data
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors surged after falling for three consecutive weeks, according to the latest survey released by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bullish sentiment as per the weekly AAII investor sentiment survey rose to 41.7% for the week ending September 17, up sharply from 28.0% in the prior update. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment declined to 42.4%, compared to 49.5% the week before.

Investors grew more confident this week amid steady inflation reports and positive developments in the tech sector, including a major chip deal between Intel and Nvidia. Central banks in Europe and the UK kept interest rates steady, which helped ease concerns about a potential recession.

While the Federal Reserve’s 25 basis points rate cut was announced only at the end of the week, it reinforced growing optimism heading into the new week.

Neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also dropped, coming in at 16.0% versus 22.5% the previous week. The decline suggests more investors are moving off the sidelines and forming clearer directional views on the market.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Rithm Capital launches preferred stock offering
data.description:
• Rithm Capital (NYSE:RITM) has launched a public offering of Series E preferred stock and plans to list it on the NYSE under the symbol “RITM PR E.”
  • Underwriters will have a 30-day option to buy up to 15% more shares. Proceeds will be used for investments and general corporate purposes.
  • RITM +0.64% premarket.

data.title: BioCardia announces pricing of up to $12M public offering
data.description:
• BioCardia (NASDAQ:BCDA) on Thursday announced the pricing of a public offering of an aggregate of 4.8M shares of its common stock and short-term warrants to purchase up to 4.8M shares of common stock, at a combined public offering price of $1.25 per share and accompanying short-term warrant.
  • The short-term warrants will have an exercise price of $1.25 per share, will be exercisable upon issuance, and will expire two years thereafter.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about September 19.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be $6 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • BCDA -26.38% premarket to $1.13.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lyft-Waymo Nashville partnership lifts shares but analysts urge caution
data.description:
On Wednesday, Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) announced a collaboration to bring autonomous ridesharing to Nashville through a partnership with Alphabet’s Waymo (GOOG), a pairing that catapulted Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) shares to a 3-year high.

The initial outsized reaction in Lyft’s (NASDAQ:LYFT) share price and subsequent retreat to a closing gain of 13% underscores the knee-jerk reaction, and later, more measured consideration of how much a partnership will actually benefit Lyft (LYFT).

Analysts on Wall Street strike a more sobering tone, noting that while the deal is beneficial, it might not remain exclusive.

At the start, Waymo (GOOG) rides will be ordered from the Waymo app and then transition to the Lyft (LYFT) platform. That said, there could be limited impact on Lyft’s (LYFT) volume growth as Waymo’s (GOOG) supply could be limited to start, says BofA Securities’ Michael McGovern.

Furthermore, McGovern fears the Lyft-Waymo partnership in Nashville might not be exclusive, and Waymo One (GOOG) will continue to offer rides directly, even after the partnership launch, while its unclear if Lyft (LYFT) will have a Waymo-only tier.

“The partnership shows the value of the Lyft network, and there is not denying this partnership speaks to Lyft’s rideshare network value in this early developing AV landscape,” says Morgan Stanley’s Brian Nowak.

"But investors need to be reminded that Nashville is a small market for Lyft (LYFT) and is not expected to impact the company’s financial model," he adds.

“Execution and incrementality will matter from here,” Nowak says, specifically, can Equal Weight-rated Lyft drive strong enough demand to the Waymo fleet and can Lyft+Waymo expand the rideshare total addressable market and drive incremental total rideshare trip volume?

And what does this mean for Uber (UBER)? The extent to which Uber (UBER) can drive consistently higher yield on the Waymo cars and incrementality to the rideshare industry in those markets “will be important to determining Uber’s ability to garner more partnerships,” Nowak says.

For BofA’s McGovern, his Underperform rating on Lyft (LYFT) underscores the lingering risks from Uber’s (UBER) dominance in this space.

“Headwinds from pricing and share losses vs Uber and AVs in likely to drive sentiment [for Lyft] lower from here, as Uber took substantial share in the ridesharing market in the post-pandemic recovery, leading to scale issues for Lyft.”

data.title: RLJ Lodging Trust names Nikhil Bhalla as CFO
data.description:
• RLJ Lodging Trust (NYSE:RLJ) has appointed Nikhil Bhalla as chief financial officer, effective immediately.
  • Since joining the company in 2015, Bhalla has held a variety of leadership positions, most recently serving as senior vice president of finance and treasurer.

data.title: N2OFF to execute 1-for-35 reverse stock split
data.description:
• N2OFF (NASDAQ:NITO) board has approved a 1-for-35 reverse stock split of its common stock that is scheduled to become effective for Nasdaq purposes on Sept. 22, 2025.
  • Upon effectiveness of the reverse stock split, every 35 shares of the company’s outstanding common stock will be converted to one share of common stock.
  • The move is intended to increase the per share price and bid price of the company’s common stock to regain compliance with the continued listing requirements of Nasdaq.
  • NITO --10.9% premarket to $0.179
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biogen to buy Massachusetts-based Alcyone Therapeutics
data.description:
Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) said that it has agreed to acquire Alcyone Therapeutics, a company based in Massachusetts.

The deal involves an upfront payment of $85M, along with additional milestone payments tied to the development and regulatory approval of ThecaFlex DRx, which is associated with nusinersen and other products in the pipeline. 

This acquisition grants Biogen full rights to ThecaFlex DRx.

Biogen and Alcyone Therapeutics are already collaborating on ThecaFlex DRx, a device designed for the subcutaneous delivery of antisense oligonucleotides. 

Once the acquisition is finalized, Alcyone employees who join Biogen will become part of the product delivery solutions team, enhancing Biogen's capabilities in drug-device combination products.

The ThecaFlex DRx system is currently being tested with Spinraza (nusinersen) in patients suffering from spinal muscular atrophy, which will help shape Biogen's future investigational therapies.

Additionally, the deal will involve convertible debt financing in Neela Therapeutics from current investors, including Biogen.

data.title: Ameresco tapped by Kimberly-Clark as engineering service provider for U.K. green hydrogen projects
data.description:
Ameresco (NYSE:AMRC) +3.1% pre-market Thursday after saying it secured a contract with Kimberly-Clark (KMB) to serve as engineering service provider for the design and construction phases of the U.K.'s first Green Hydrogen Program in the consumer goods sector.

Ameresco (NYSE:AMRC) said it will develop the installation of five new dual-fuel hydrogen and natural gas boilers to enable effective hydrogen consumption while maintaining a backup from natural gas at Kimberly-Clark's (KMB) plants in Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria, and Northfleet, Kent.

The company expects the dual-fuel boilers to become operational in 2027 and are projected to reduce natural gas consumption by 50%, resulting in a decrease in carbon emissions of 28,500 metric tons/year.

The collaboration marks a significant milestone for Kimberly-Clark (KMB) as the first major consumer goods company in the U.K. to commit significantly to green hydrogen technology at its plants and for the U.K.'s adoption of green hydrogen technology in the industrial landscape.
